test_case_1_partial_brd.content:
FIRSTCAPITAL BANK — PERSONAL LOAN PRODUCT
Integration Requirements v1.2

1. APPLICANT VERIFICATION
All loan applicants must be verified using Aadhaar-based eKYC before proceeding to any other step. KYC is mandatory for all loan types.
Applicant fields available: applicant_name, applicant_dob, applicant_phone, applicant_aadhaar_ref (masked), consent_token

2. CREDIT ASSESSMENT
Fetch CIBIL bureau report for all applicants. Bureau fetch should only happen after KYC is successfully completed.
Loan amounts above Rs 5,00,000 require Experian report additionally.

3. FRAUD SCREENING
[SECTION INTENTIONALLY LEFT BLANK - TO BE UPDATED]

4. PAYMENT DISBURSEMENT
Use Razorpay for all loan disbursements via NEFT/IMPS.
Disbursement should only happen after credit assessment is complete and loan is approved by underwriter.

test_case_2_amendment_brd.document_v1_content:
GROWTHFINANCE — PERSONAL LOAN PRODUCT
Initial Integration Requirements

KYC VERIFICATION
All applicants verified via Aadhaar eKYC. Mandatory.

CREDIT ASSESSMENT
CIBIL v3.0 bureau fetch required after KYC completion.

FRAUD SCREENING
Fraud screening recommended for loans above Rs 2,00,000 (optional).

PAYMENT DISBURSEMENT
Razorpay payment gateway for all disbursements.
Disbursement after final approval.
test_case_2_amendment_brd.document_v2_content:
GROWTHFINANCE — AMENDED INTEGRATION REQUIREMENTS
Amendment Notice — Integration Change Request #47
Effective Immediately

CHANGE 1: FRAUD SCREENING NOW MANDATORY
FraudShield v2.0 integration is now MANDATORY for all loan applications.
Previous: Optional for loans above Rs 2L
Now: Mandatory for ALL applications regardless of amount
Trigger: Regulatory requirement

CHANGE 2: NEW GST REQUIREMENT
GST verification required for all business loan applicants.
Provider: GSTN official API
Mandatory: Yes, for applicant_type == 'business'
Field available: applicant_gstin
Must run in parallel with bureau fetch, not sequentially.

CHANGE 3-5: KYC, Bureau, Payment UNCHANGED

test_case_3_regulatory_brd.content:
REGIONALCREDIT NBFC
Digital Lending Integration Requirements
Compliance Reference: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2023, Clause 7.4

SECTION A: DATA LOCALIZATION AND CONSENT
Per RBI DLG 2023 Clause 7.4, all borrower data obtained through third-party integrations must have explicit digital consent recorded prior to data fetch. Consent must be AA-framework compliant where applicable. Integration with the Account Aggregator ecosystem is preferred for financial data but not exclusive.

SECTION B: CREDIT DECISIONING
The platform shall integrate with at least one RBI-recognized credit information company. CIBIL TransUnion is preferred but Equifax or Experian may be used as fallback. Bureau pull must not happen more than once per application. Hard inquiry rules apply per CIC regulations.

In case of thin-file applicants (bureau score unavailable or below threshold), alternative data sources including GST returns and bank statement analysis via AA shall be used for credit assessment. Thin-file threshold: score less than 600 or no bureau history.

SECTION C: IDENTITY VERIFICATION  
KYC must comply with the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) and RBI KYC Master Directions 2016. Full KYC is required. Video KYC is acceptable as per RBI circular RBI/2019-20/170.
Aadhaar OTP-based KYC is acceptable for amounts up to Rs 50,000 only.
For amounts above Rs 50,000, Video KYC or full Aadhaar biometric required.

SECTION D: FRAUD AND RISK
Implement fraud controls as per internal risk policy. (See Risk Policy Document v3.2 — not attached)
Real-time fraud scoring is recommended.

SECTION E: CONTRADICTING CLAUSE
Section B states bureau pull must not happen more than once.
Section D references real-time fraud scoring which in some implementations uses soft bureau pulls. This conflict to be resolved by Risk team.
Note: FraudShield v1.5 integration is preferred by compliance team.
(Note: FraudShield v1.5 does not exist in adapter catalog)
